rulings.Case Title: JOHN T. PRIEST VS. STARBUCKS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order And Monetary Sanctions As To Pltff'S Ntc Of Depositions And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 17, 2013, Line 2. Defendant STARBUCKS CORPORATION dba STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY'S Motion for Protective Order and Monetary Sanctions as to Plaintiff's Notice of Depositions and Request for Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KATHY BOUSSINA AS ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF et al VS. SHEILA MANCHESTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 17, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.' The motion is granted. The motion is granted as it relates to claims for punitive damages, exemplary damages, treble damages, and attorney's fees in the second cause of action for financial elder abuse. Because Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend paragraphs 31, 33, 35 and 37 are already stricken from the Second Amended Complaint. Consequently, paragraph 7 of Plaintiff's prayer for relief, seeking attorney fees under Welf. & Inst. Code 15657, is also stricken. Defendant's motion to strike paragraph 50 from cause of action four, and paragraphs 3 and 6 in the prayer for relief, is granted with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs are given 10 days leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, they can plead facts with specificity that Defendant's officer, director, or managing agent authorized or ratified the alleged wrongful conduct. See Cal Civ. Code § 3294(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: KATHY BOUSSINA AS ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF et al VS. SHEILA MANCHESTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 17, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The allegation that Defendant "took, secreted, and appropriated" Plaintiffs' decedent's personal property fails as a matter of law. Defendant did not take Plaintiffs' decedent money, as she voluntarily entered into a settlement agreement with Defendant for less than the policy amount. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: INGHAM, ERICA ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. CLUB ONE, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 17, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANTs CLUB ONE, INC., A CORPORATION, BILL MCBRIDE, ADRIENNE MCBRIDE and CINDY KIRKMAN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Club One, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Adjudication of the First Cause of Action in Plaintiff's Complaint is granted. Plaintiff is required to exhaust her administrative remedies under Labor Code § 98.7 before filing suit for a violation of Labor Code § 1102.5 and failed to do so. See Campbell v. Regents of the University of California (2005) 35 Cal.4th 311; Ferretti v. Pfizer Inc. (N.D.Cal. 2012) 855 F.Supp.2d 1017. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with Code of Civ. Pro. section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMBILE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MICHAEL ANTHONY TEJADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 17, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT TOMASICO YAMBAO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is overruled. Taking the facts in the Complaint as true, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to constitute a claim of equitable subrogation. (See Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Maryland Cas. Co. (1998) 65 Cal. App. 4th 1279, 1292.) Defendant's demurrer that the Complaint is uncertain is overruled. Taking the facts as true, Defendant Yambao can reasonably determine what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts and claims are directed against him or her. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER CALVI VS. VITO J. LBERATI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 17, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action and overruled as to the fifth cause of action. For the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff has not alleged a duty owed by the defendant to the plaintiff or a breach of that duty. (In re Cooper/T. Smith (5th Cir. 1991) 929 F.2d 1073, 1077; Complaint 13, 28, 31. For the fifth cause of action, Defendant raises questions of fact regarding Plaintiff's relationship to Defendant that are not the proper subject of a demurrer. Plaintiff is given leave to amend only to allege a duty owed by the defendant to the plaintiff and a breach of that duty. Plaintiff's request to add a sixth cause of action in his opposition is denied as procedurally inappropriate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)
